saints.name: Saint Melchizedek I, Catholicos-Patriarch of All Georgia
saints.excerpt: After the repose of Catholicos Simeon, leadership of the Georgian Church passed to Catholicos Melchizedek I. saints.excerpt: Saint Hilarion of Gdov and Pskov Lake, was a disciple of Saint Euphrosynus of Pskov (May 15). saints.excerpt: The Martyrs Gaius, Dasius, and Zoticus of Nicomedia suffered martyrdom in the year 303, under the emperor Diocletian (284-305), because they destroyed a pagan temple. After enduring many tortures, they had stones tied around their necks, and were drowned in the sea.

saints.name: Venerable Bessarion Sarai the Confessor in Romania
saints.excerpt: Saint Bessarion (Sarai) was a Serb who was born in Bosnia in 1714. Longing for the monastic life, he was tonsured at the Monastery of Saint Savva in the Holy Land in 1738. He returned to Serbia and lived in a cave for several years as a hesychast, and received from God the grace of working…

saints.name: Saint Sophronius, Confessor of Ciorara, Romania
saints.excerpt: Saint Sophronius was originally from Ciorara-Sebesh in Alba county in Romania. From his childhood, he demonstrated a great love for Christ and the Church, so it was not surprising that he eventually received the monastic tonsure. He returned to his village in 1756, and established a small hermitage…

saints.name: Martyr Oprea of Salistie in Romania
saints.excerpt: Saint Oprea Nicholas of Salistie suffered martyrdom in Romania at the hands of Roman Catholics in 1776.

saints.name: Saint Hilarion, Metropolitan of Kiev
saints.excerpt: Saint Hilarion, Metropolitan of Kiev, lived during the era of the Great Prince Yaroslav the Wise (+ 1054), son of Saint Vladimir. saints.name: Martyr Moses (Macinic) the Confessor
saints.excerpt: Saint Moses Macinic was ordained to the holy priesthood in Bucharest around 1746, and worked to oppose the Unia. saints.name: New Martyr John of Monemvasia
saints.excerpt: Saint John was born in 1758 near the city of Monemvasia. His father was a priest from Geraki, and his mother was from the neighboring village of Gouves. It was in Gouves that John's father was assigned as a priest, and there John was born. From a young age John tried to emulate his father by…

saints.name: 7 Holy Youths “Seven Sleepers” of Ephesus
saints.excerpt: The Seven Youths of Ephesus: Maximilian, Iamblicus, Martinian, John, Dionysius, Exacustodianus (Constantine) and Antoninus, lived in the third century. Saint Maximilian was the son of the Ephesus city administrator, and the other six youths were sons of illustrious citizens of Ephesus. The youths…

saints.name: Hieromartyr Alexander, Bishop of Adrianopolis, and the Martyrs Heraclius, Anna, Elizabeth, Theodota, and Glyceria
saints.excerpt: The third century century was a time when Christianity experienced significant growth among those who worshipped idols. Many of these were killed simply because they believed in Christ and refused to worship idols. Saint Alexander was the Bishop of Adrianopolis, who proclaimed the Gospel with…

saints.excerpt: The Commemoration of the Deliverance of Moscow From the Poles by the Kazan Icon was established in gratitude for the deliverance of Moscow and all Russia from the incursion of the Polish in 1612. saints.excerpt: The Andronicus Icon of the Mother of God was a family icon of the Byzantine emperor Andronicus III. In 1347 he gave the icon to the Monemvasia monastery at Morea in the Peloponnesos. From here the image was sent to Russia in 1839. In 1877 the holy icon was placed in a temple of the Kazan…

saints.name: Saints Paul and Theodore of Rostov
saints.excerpt: Saints Paul and Theodore of Rostov founded a monastery at the River Ust, not far from Rostov, in honor of the holy Martyrs Boris and Gleb (May 2). Saint Theodore (November 28) first came to the site of the future monastery from the Novgorod region. Saint Paul came three years later for ascetic…

saints.name: Venerable Lot of Egypt
saints.excerpt: Saint Lot was a wise ascetic of the desert who reposed in peace in the early fifth century. Some of his sayings are to be found in the "Paradise of the Fathers," which describes the life of the anchorites, recluses, monks, coenobites and ascetic Fathers of the deserts of Egypt compiled by Saint…

saints.excerpt: The Holy Apostle James, the Brother of God (Adelphótheos) was the son of the Righteous Joseph before he was betrothed to the Most Holy Theotokos. Tradition says that Joseph had other sons with his first wife. Thus, James was called the Lord's brother. Saint James had been a Nazirite, a man or…

saints.excerpt: Saint Ignatius, Patriarch of Constantinople (847-857; 867-877), in the world Nicetas, was of imperial lineage. While living at the monastery, he kept many possessions in his cell. One day robbers made off with it. Grieving over his…

saints.name: Blessed Elesbaan, King of Ethiopia
saints.excerpt: Blessed Elesbaan1 was the Emperor of what is now Ethiopia, and lived when Arabia was ruled by Dunaan, an oppressor of Christians. saints.name: Martyr Syncletica and her two daughters
saints.excerpt: The Martyr Syncletica and her two daughters suffered under the Arabian king Dunaan. Saint Syncletica was a descendant of an illustrious family. saints.name: Martyr Anastasius the Fuller at Salona in Dalmatia
saints.excerpt: The Martyr Anastasius the Fuller lived at Salona in Dalmatia during the third century. He was arrested and brought to trial because of his missionary activity in Salona. Saint Anastasius, boldly and without fear, confessed Christ as the true God and Creator of all. He even painted a cross on his…

saints.name: Saint Tabitha the Widow, raised from the dead by the Apostle Peter
saints.excerpt: Saint Tabitha, the widow raised from the dead by the Apostle Peter, was a virtuous and kindly woman who belonged to the Christian community in Joppa. saints.name: Venerable Athanasius of Medikion Monastery

saints.excerpt: Saint Melitina (Melitēnḗ) was accused of being a Christian and taken to the local ruler, before whom she confessed her faith in Christ. saints.name: Apostle Tertius of the Seventy
saints.excerpt: Saint Tertius was the second bishop (after Saint Sosipater) in Iconium, where he converted many pagans to Christ, and ended his life as a martyr. The Apostle Paul mentions him in the Epistle to the Romans (Rom. 16:22).

saints.name: Apostle Mark of the Seventy
saints.excerpt: Saint Mark, also called John, (Acts 12:12), was a nephew of Saint Barnabas, and was Bishop of Apollonia (Col. 4:10). It was in the house of his mother Maria that the persecuted disciples found shelter after the Ascension of the Lord.

saints.name: Apostle Justus of the Seventy
saints.excerpt: Saint Justus, called Barsaba, a son of Saint Joseph the Betrothed, was chosen with Matthias to replace Judas. He was a bishop and died a martyr’s death at Eleutheropolis.

saints.name: Apostle Artemas of the Seventy
saints.excerpt: Saint Artemas was bishop of Lystra, Lycia. He died in peace.

saints.name: Hieromartyr Marcian, Bishop of Syracuse
saints.excerpt: The Holy Hieromartyr Marcian, Bishop of Syracuse, a disciple of the Apostle Peter, was sent to Sicily. saints.name: Martyr Eutropia of Alexandria
saints.excerpt: The Martyr Eutropia suffered for Christ in Alexandria in about the year 250. Often visiting Christians locked up in prison, she encouraged them to endure suffering with patience. For this, the saint was arrested. At her trial she firmly confessed her faith in Christ. As she was being burned with…

saints.name: Martyr Anastasia of Thessalonica
saints.excerpt: Saint Anastasia lived in the second half of the third century during the persecutions of Decius, Gallus, Valerian, and Diocletian. She was executed in Rome between 256-259 after enduring many tortures.

saints.excerpt: Saint Stephen was the younger son of King Stephen Urosh I, and grandson of First-Crowned King Saint Stephen (September 24). He ruled Serbia from 1275 to 1320. saints.excerpt: Saint Dragutin was the brother of Saint Stephen Milutin, the son of King Stephen Urosh I, and the grandson of First-Crowned King Saint Stephen (September 24). saints.name: Saint Helen of Serbia
saints.excerpt: Saint Helen, a pious mother to her sons Stephen Milutin and Dragutin, devoted her whole life to pious deeds after the death of her husband. saints.name: Apostle Aristobulus of the Seventy
saints.excerpt: Saint Aristobulus, one of the Seventy Apostles, was the brother of Saint Barnabus (June 11) and proclaimed the Gospel in Britain, where he died in peace. He is also commemorated on March 16.

saints.name: Venerable Maura of Constantinople
saints.excerpt: Saint Maura (Μαύρα) was a Deaconess and Igoumeness. She labored in asceticism during the first half of the fifth century at Constantinople, where founded a monastery, in which she reposed. In Greek usage Saint Maura is commemorated on November 9.
